explanation.rule: Negative prefixes are added to the beginning of words to create opposite or negative meanings.

groups.note: The prefix im- is a form of in- used before words beginning with b, m, or p. It gives the word an opposite or negative meaning.

examples.definition: not able to happen or be done
not suitable for use in real situations

wordForms.definition: not able to move or be moved
wordForms.example: After the accident, he was completely immobile for three weeks.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to move or be moved freely

wordForms.definition: not following accepted moral or ethical standards
wordForms.example: The journalist claimed the company's business practices were immoral.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to right and wrong conduct

wordForms.definition: not willing to wait calmly; easily annoyed by delays
wordForms.example: She was impatient to hear the results of the interview.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to wait calmly without complaining

wordForms.example: It would be impolite to leave without saying goodbye.
wordForms.rootDefinition: showing good manners and consideration for others

wordForms.definition: not able to happen, exist, or be done
wordForms.example: It's impossible to finish this report by tomorrow morning.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to happen, exist, or be done

wordForms.definition: not suitable or effective in real-life situations
wordForms.example: The proposed design looks attractive but is completely impractical.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to actual use; effective in real situations

wordForms.definition: not following accepted rules or standards; inappropriate
wordForms.example: Wearing a tracksuit to a formal dinner would be improper.
wordForms.rootDefinition: following the correct rules and accepted standards

wordForms.example: The water supply was found to be impure and unsafe to drink.
wordForms.rootDefinition: not mixed with anything else; clean and uncontaminated

wordForms.definition: a situation where things are not equal or fair
wordForms.example: There is a significant imbalance between the pay of men and women in the sector.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a state where things are equal or in proportion

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: It is ___ to deliver the whole project in just two days.
The company's tactics were widely regarded as ___.
She grew ___ waiting for the train that was already forty minutes late.
It would be ___ to interrupt the speaker mid-sentence.
There is a serious ___ between the number of jobs available and graduates seeking work.
The design is beautiful but ___ for everyday use.

transformExercises.sentence: Finishing the work tonight seems completely ___. (POSSIBLE)
After the fall, he remained ___ for several minutes. (MOBILE)
He was ___ to hear the results and kept checking his phone. (PATIENT)
Wearing jeans to the awards ceremony would be considered ___. (PROPER)
There is a clear ___ of power between the two departments. (BALANCE)
